title: Titration Curve Comparison Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive titration curve visualizer with annotations for equivalence, buffer regions, and indicator suitability across strong and weak combinations.

learningObjectives: Interpret key regions of titration curves (initial region, buffer region, half-equivalence, equivalence).
Predict indicator suitability by comparing transition ranges with the titration curve's steep jump.

keyQuestions: How does the equivalence pH shift between strong and weak titration pairs?
Which indicators overlap the rapid pH change for a selected titration?
commonMisconceptions: All equivalence points occur at pH 7.
Diluting both solutions changes the equivalence volume.
assessmentOpportunities: Learners submit annotated plots with justification of indicator choices.
Students explain why the buffer region is absent for strong acid-strong base titrations.
